summary: Austrian Expressionist painter and playwright. Most commonly identified by The Bride of the Wind (The Tempest), depicting himself and Alma Mahler. Known for the infamous life-size sex doll of Alma. Also a playwright: Murderer, Hope of Women; Orpheus and Eurydice. Student of Klimt; Vienna Secession.

works.name: The Bride of the Wind / The Tempest (1914)
works.description: Shows Kokoschka lying wide awake as dark waves swirl around him, with sleeping Alma Mahler in his arms. Based on their tempestuous affair.

clues.clue: Dark waves swirl around him as he lies wide awake with his sleeping lover (Alma Mahler) in his arms
examples: dark waves swirl around him as he lies wide awake with his sleeping lover in his arms
The Bride of the Wind shows him sleeping next to Alma Mahler

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: How Kokoschka is identified and biographical clues.

drunkenly decapitated a life-sized doll in her likeness at a party

clues.clue: Self-Portrait as Knight Errant: sphinx at right of stormy landscape; inspired by Alma's abortion
examples: A sphinx appears at the right of a stormy landscape in a self-portrait of this man as the titular Knight Errant, inspired by his lover's abortion

clues.clue: The Dreaming Boys: lithograph series of sexual awakening in pastel colors
examples: used pastel red, blue, green, and yellow for a lithograph series illustrating sexual awakening, entitled The Dreaming Boys

clues.clue: Murderer, Hope of Women (play): 'The Man' is stabbed by 'The Woman' after entering a dark tower; Man kills Woman with a single touch

clues.clue: Student of Klimt; painted portraits for architect Adolf Loos's clients
examples: a student of Klimt, painted a later portrait of Adele's husband Ferdinand as well as many portraits of the clients of architect Adolf Loos

clues.clue: Vienna Secession member; art deemed 'degenerate' by Nazis
examples: Beckmann's art was deemed "degenerate," as was the art of this Viennese artist

clues.clue: Painting of Queen Victoria holding a frog and riding a shark; British sailors drown, octopus carries trident
examples: An octopus carries away a trident and some British sailors drown in a painting by this artist in which Queen Victoria holds a frog and rides a shark

clues.clue: Wrote play Orpheus and Eurydice (adapted into opera by Ernst Krenek) after WWI service in Ukraine
examples: After serving in Ukraine during World War I, he wrote a play converted into an opera by Ernst Krenek about Orpheus and Eurydice

clues.clue: Play Hiob/Job adapted from earlier Sphinx and Strawman; Mr. Rubberman emerges from dog's skin
examples: sees Mr. Rubberman emerge from a dog's skin before confronting the title character

clues.clue: O Eternity, Thou Word of Thunder; Loreley; Bach cantata-inspired series Fear guides to the grave
examples: Fear guides this man, as Hope, to his grave in a series he drew after hearing a friend play a Bach cantata

clues.clue: Seven Chinese fans documenting Italy trip with Alma; last thrown into fire in jealous fit
examples: A journey to Italy...is documented in the third of a series of seven Chinese fans, the last of which was thrown into a fire in a fit of jealousy

clues.clue: Kept blood-stained cotton pad from Alma's abortion until his death; later proposed making a puppet with erect penis for 80-year-old Alma
examples: kept in his possession a blood-stained cotton pad the other used when she had an abortion
When one of these people was 80 years old, the other wrote to her proposing he make a wooden puppet of himself equipped with an erect penis

recursive_suggestions: Alma Mahler — cross-topic figure (married Gustav Mahler, Walter Gropius)

comprehensive_summary:
Oskar Kokoschka was an Austrian Expressionist painter and playwright. His most famous painting, The Bride of the Wind (also called The Tempest, 1914), shows him lying wide awake with dark waves swirling around him as sleeping Alma Mahler rests in his arms. Their tempestuous affair produced many works and incidents: he commissioned a life-size sex doll of Alma, which he drunkenly decapitated at a party. He kept a blood-stained cotton pad from Alma's abortion until his death. When Alma was 80, he proposed making a wooden puppet of himself with an erect penis. A series of seven Chinese fans documented their Italian journey, the last thrown into fire in jealousy.

His Self-Portrait as Knight Errant features a sphinx in a stormy landscape, inspired by Alma's abortion. The Dreaming Boys is a lithograph series of sexual awakening. He painted Queen Victoria holding a frog and riding a shark while British sailors drown. He was a student of Klimt and painted portraits for architect Adolf Loos's clients. He was a Vienna Secession member whose art was deemed 'degenerate.'

As a playwright, Kokoschka wrote Murderer, Hope of Women (in which 'The Man' kills 'The Woman' with a single touch) and Hiob/Job (adapted from Sphinx and Strawman). His play about Orpheus and Eurydice was converted into an opera by Ernst Krenek.
